library_item_preview.title: NAIC Letter to Senate Appropriators on SHIP and MHP Grant Funding

fields.Name: NAIC Letter to Senate Appropriators on SHIP and MHP Grant Funding
fields.Description: This document is an NAIC letter dated May 1, 2025, to U.S. Senate leadership and Senate Appropriations Committee leadership urging full FY2026 funding for State Health Insurance Assistance Programs (SHIPs) and for state enforcement grants under the Mental Health Parity and Addiction Equity Act of 2008 (MHPAEA). It explains the NAIC's concerns about proposed SHIP funding elimination and advocates at least $10 million for MHPAEA state enforcement grants.

field_ids.name_text: NAIC Letter to Senate Appropriators on SHIP and MHP Grant Funding
field_ids.description_text: This document is an NAIC letter dated May 1, 2025, to U.S. Senate leadership and Senate Appropriations Committee leadership urging full FY2026 funding for State Health Insurance Assistance Programs (SHIPs) and for state enforcement grants under the Mental Health Parity and Addiction Equity Act of 2008 (MHPAEA). It explains the NAIC's concerns about proposed SHIP funding elimination and advocates at least $10 million for MHPAEA state enforcement grants.

library_item_preview.title: NAIC Letter to House Appropriators on SHIP and MHP Grant Funding

fields.Name: NAIC Letter to House Appropriators on SHIP and MHP Grant Funding
fields.Description: A May 1, 2025 NAIC letter to U.S. House leadership and House Appropriations Committee leaders urging full FY2026 funding for State Health Insurance Assistance Programs (SHIPs) and for state enforcement grants under the Mental Health Parity and Addiction Equity Act of 2008 (MHPAEA). The letter explains the consumer-protection rationale for both appropriations priorities and highlights state regulators' concerns about proposed federal funding changes.

field_ids.name_text: NAIC Letter to House Appropriators on SHIP and MHP Grant Funding
field_ids.description_text: A May 1, 2025 NAIC letter to U.S. House leadership and House Appropriations Committee leaders urging full FY2026 funding for State Health Insurance Assistance Programs (SHIPs) and for state enforcement grants under the Mental Health Parity and Addiction Equity Act of 2008 (MHPAEA). The letter explains the consumer-protection rationale for both appropriations priorities and highlights state regulators' concerns about proposed federal funding changes.

agenda_item_title_chronicle_topics.agenda_item_title: Reinsurance collateral treatment of derecognized net negative IMR

agenda_item_title_official.official_title: The purpose of this meeting is to adopt a recommendation to the Statutory Accounting Principles (E) Working Group on the issue of how to treat derecognized net negative IMR (realized losses) in relation to reinsurance collateral required for applicable unauthorized or certified reinsurers.

library_item_preview.title: Agenda and Materials for June 22, 2026 Reinsurance (E) Task Force Meeting

fields.Name: Agenda and Materials for June 22, 2026 Reinsurance (E) Task Force Meeting
fields.Description: This document is the agenda and meeting materials for the June 22, 2026 virtual meeting of the Reinsurance (E) Task Force. It includes the meeting logistics, roll call, agenda, and a draft response from the Reinsurance (E) Task Force to the Statutory Accounting Principles (E) Working Group regarding derecognized IMR impact to reinsurance collateral (Ref #2025-22).
